---
title: "Final"
author: "Nikita, Trilok, Deekshita"
date: "2024-12-10"
output: html_document
---

#reading Exchange Rates data

```{r}
library(readxl)

Australia  <- read_excel("Exchange Rate - AUS.xls",skip = 10)
Canada <- read_excel("Exchange Rate - Canada.xls",skip = 10)
Euro <- read_excel("Exchange Rate - Euro.xls",skip = 10)
Japan<- read_excel("Exchange Rate - Japan.xls",skip = 10)
UK <- read_excel("Exchange Rate - UK.xls",skip = 10)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Create a country code lookup table
country_codes <- data.frame(
  Country = c("Australia", "Canada", "Euro", "Japan", "UK"),
  Country_ID = 1:5  # Assign numbers alphabetically
)

# Add the Country_ID column to each dataset
Australia <- Australia %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

Canada <- Canada %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

Euro <- Euro %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

Japan <- Japan %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UK <- UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

# Check the result for one dataset (e.g., Australia)
head(Australia)
head(Canada)
```
```{r}

# Rename columns in each dataset to make them consistent
Exchangerates_Australia <- Australia %>%
  rename( ExchangeRate = `DEXUSAL`) %>%  # Replace 'Date' and 'Rate' with actual names
  mutate(Country = "Australia")


Exchangerates_Canada <- Canada %>%
  rename( ExchangeRate = `DEXCAUS`) %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada")

Exchangerates_Euro <- Euro %>%
  rename(ExchangeRate = `DEXUSEU`) %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro")


Exchangerates_Japan <- Japan %>%
  rename( ExchangeRate = `DEXJPUS`) %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan")


Exchangerates_UK <- UK %>%
  rename( ExchangeRate = `DEXUSUK`) %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Rearrange columns in each dataset
Exchangerates_Australia <- Exchangerates_Australia %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, ExchangeRate)

Exchangerates_Canada <- Exchangerates_Canada %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, ExchangeRate)

Exchangerates_Euro <- Exchangerates_Euro %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, ExchangeRate)

Exchangerates_Japan <- Exchangerates_Japan %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, ExchangeRate)

Exchangerates_UK <- Exchangerates_UK %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, ExchangeRate)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Function to calculate percentage difference
calculate_percentage_difference <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Ensure data is sorted by date
    mutate(ERPC = (ExchangeRate - lag(ExchangeRate)) / lag(ExchangeRate))
}

# Apply the function to each dataset
Exchangerates_Australia <- calculate_percentage_difference(Exchangerates_Australia)
Exchangerates_Canada <- calculate_percentage_difference(Exchangerates_Canada)
Exchangerates_Euro <- calculate_percentage_difference(Exchangerates_Euro)
Exchangerates_Japan <- calculate_percentage_difference(Exchangerates_Japan)
Exchangerates_UK <- calculate_percentage_difference(Exchangerates_UK)

# Preview the datasets
head(Exchangerates_Australia)
head(Exchangerates_Canada)
head(Exchangerates_Euro)
head(Exchangerates_Japan)
head(Exchangerates_UK)
```
```{r}
# Combine all datasets by stacking them as rows
combined_exchange_data <- bind_rows(
  Exchangerates_Australia,
  Exchangerates_Canada,
  Exchangerates_Euro,
  Exchangerates_Japan,
  Exchangerates_UK
)

# Arrange the combined data by 'observation_date' and 'CountryCode' (optional)
combined_exchange_data <- combined_exchange_data %>%
  arrange(Country_ID, observation_date)

# Remove rows where ExchangeRate is 0
combined_exchange_data <- combined_exchange_data %>%
  filter(ExchangeRate != 0)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(combined_exchange_data)

# Check if any rows with ExchangeRate == 0 remain
sum(combined_exchange_data$ExchangeRate == 0)

# Preview the combined data
head(combined_exchange_data)
```
#changing date format
```{r}
library(readxl)
SP500 <- read_excel("SP500.xlsx")

head(SP500)
# Check the column names of the SP500 dataset
colnames(SP500)
# Ensure the 'date' column is converted to Date type and formatted as "YYYY-MM-DD"
SP500$Date <- format(as.Date(SP500$Date, format = "%d-%b-%y"), "%Y-%m-%d")

# Check the result to ensure the format change was successful
head(SP500)

# Keep only the "Date" and "Adj Close" columns using dplyr
library(dplyr)

SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  select(Date, `Adj Close `)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(SP500)

# Rename "Adj Close" to "SP500"
colnames(SP500)[2] <- "SP500"

#date to "observation_date"
colnames(SP500)[1] <- "observation_date"

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)

# Create a daily date sequence for SP500
SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%  # Ensure date format
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(observation_date), by = "day"))  # Fill missing dates


SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  mutate(SP500 = zoo::na.approx(SP500, na.rm = FALSE)) %>%  # Interpolate missing values
  mutate(SP500 = ifelse(is.na(SP500),
                        zoo::na.locf(SP500, fromLast = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Forward fill
                        SP500)) %>%
  mutate(SP500 = ifelse(is.na(SP500),
                        zoo::na.locf(SP500, fromLast = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Backward fill
                        SP500))

# Preview the updated dataset
head(SP500)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Ensure SP500 values are numeric (if not already)
SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  mutate(SP500 = as.numeric(SP500))

# Calculate the percentage change
SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Ensure data is sorted by date
  mutate(
    SP500PC = (SP500 - lag(SP500)) / lag(SP500)   # Percentage change formula
  )

# Preview the updated dataset
head(SP500)
```
#reading and updating the VIX data
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
VIX <- read_excel("VIX.xls",skip = 10)

# Remove the time component from the observation_date column
VIX$observation_date <- as.Date(VIX$observation_date)

VIX <- VIX %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%  # Ensure date format
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(observation_date), by = "day"))  # Fill missing dates

VIX <- VIX %>%
  mutate(VIXCLS = ifelse(VIXCLS == 0, NA, VIXCLS)) %>%  # Convert 0 to NA for processing
  mutate(VIXCLS = zoo::na.approx(VIXCLS, na.rm = FALSE)) %>%  # Interpolate missing values
  mutate(VIXCLS = ifelse(is.na(VIXCLS),
                         zoo::na.locf(VIXCLS, fromLast = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Forward fill
                         VIXCLS)) %>%
  mutate(VIXCLS = ifelse(is.na(VIXCLS),
                         zoo::na.locf(VIXCLS, fromLast = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Backward fill
                         VIXCLS))


head(VIX)
```

```{r}
# Calculate the relative difference for the VIX values
VIX <- VIX %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Ensure the data is sorted by date
  mutate(
    VIXPC = (VIXCLS - lag(VIXCLS)) / lag(VIXCLS)   # Replace "VIX" with the correct column name
  )

# Preview the updated dataset with the new column
head(VIX)
```

#reading crude oil data
```{r}

crudeoil <- read_excel("Crude Oil WTI.xlsx")
print(crudeoil)
colnames(crudeoil)

```
#deleting columns in crude oil and keeping date and adj close

```{r}
# Select Date and Adj Close columns
crudeoil <- crudeoil %>%
  select(observation_date = Date, crude_oil = `Adj Close `)

crudeoil <- crudeoil %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%  # Ensure date format
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(observation_date), by = "day"))  # Fill missing dates

crudeoil <- crudeoil %>%
  mutate(crude_oil = zoo::na.approx(crude_oil, na.rm = FALSE)) %>%  # Interpolate missing values
  mutate(crude_oil = ifelse(is.na(crude_oil),
                            zoo::na.locf(crude_oil, fromLast = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Forward fill
                            crude_oil)) %>%
  mutate(crude_oil = ifelse(is.na(crude_oil),
                            zoo::na.locf(crude_oil, fromLast = TRUE, na.rm = FALSE),  # Backward fill
                            crude_oil))

# View cleaned data
head(crudeoil)

```
```{r}
# Calculate the percentage change
crudeoil <- crudeoil %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Ensure data is sorted by Date
  mutate(
  crudeoilPC= (crude_oil - lag(crude_oil)) / lag(crude_oil)   # Percentage change formula
  )

# View the updated dataset
head(crudeoil)
```
```{r}
#Unemployment rate

#reading Unemployment rate Data

library(readxl)
UNEMP_AUSTRALIA <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - Australia.xls",skip = 10)
UNEMP_CANADA <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - Canada.xls",skip = 10)
UNEMP_EURO <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - Euro.xls",skip = 10)
UNEMP_JAPAN <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - Japan.xls",skip = 10)
UNEMP_UK <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - UK.xls",skip = 10)
UNEMP_US <- read_excel("Unemployment Rate - US.xls",skip = 10)

print(UNEMP_AUSTRALIA)



library(dplyr)

# Create a country code lookup table
country_codes <- data.frame(
  Country = c("Australia", "Canada", "Euro", "Japan", "UK", "US"),
  Country_ID = 1:6  # Assign numbers alphabetically
)

# Add the Country_ID column to each unemployment dataset
UNEMP_AUSTRALIA <- UNEMP_AUSTRALIA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UNEMP_CANADA <- UNEMP_CANADA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UNEMP_EURO <- UNEMP_EURO %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UNEMP_JAPAN <- UNEMP_JAPAN %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UNEMP_UK <- UNEMP_UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

UNEMP_US <- UNEMP_US %>%
  mutate(Country = "US") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

# Check the result for one dataset (e.g., UNEMP_AUSTRALIA)
head(UNEMP_AUSTRALIA)
print(UNEMP_CANADA)
colnames(UNEMP_AUSTRALIA)
colnames(UNEMP_CANADA)
colnames(UNEMP_EURO)
colnames(UNEMP_UK)



library(dplyr)

# Rename the column in UNEMP_AUSTRALIA
UNEMP_AUSTRALIA <- UNEMP_AUSTRALIA %>%
  rename(`Unemployment Rate` = LRUNTTTTAUM156S)

# Repeat for other datasets
UNEMP_CANADA <- UNEMP_CANADA %>%
  rename(`Unemployment Rate` = LRUNTTTTCAM156S)

UNEMP_EURO <- UNEMP_EURO %>%
  rename(`Unemployment Rate` = LRHUTTTTEZM156S)

UNEMP_JAPAN <- UNEMP_JAPAN %>%
  rename(`Unemployment Rate` = LRHUTTTTJPM156S)

UNEMP_UK <- UNEMP_UK %>%
  rename(`Unemployment Rate` = LRHUTTTTGBM156S)

UNEMP_US <- UNEMP_US %>%
  rename(`US_UnemploymentRate` = UNRATE)

# Preview one dataset to ensure the change
head(UNEMP_AUSTRALIA)
head(UNEMP_CANADA)




library(dplyr)

# Function to calculate percentage changes and relative differences
calculate_relative_difference <- function(df, country_name) {
  df %>%
    left_join(UNEMP_US, by = "observation_date") %>%  # Add US unemployment rate
    arrange(observation_date) %>%
    mutate(
      Country = country_name,
      PercentageChange_Country = (lag(`Unemployment Rate`) - `Unemployment Rate`) / lag(`Unemployment Rate`) * 100,
      PercentageChange_US = (lag(US_UnemploymentRate) - US_UnemploymentRate) / lag(US_UnemploymentRate) * 100,
      RelativeDifference_Unemp =  PercentageChange_US-PercentageChange_Country  # Relative difference
    )
}

# Apply the function to each dataset
UNEMP_AUSTRALIA <- calculate_relative_difference(UNEMP_AUSTRALIA, "Australia")
UNEMP_CANADA <- calculate_relative_difference(UNEMP_CANADA, "Canada")
UNEMP_EURO <- calculate_relative_difference(UNEMP_EURO, "Euro")
UNEMP_JAPAN <- calculate_relative_difference(UNEMP_JAPAN, "Japan")
UNEMP_UK <- calculate_relative_difference(UNEMP_UK, "UK")

# Combine all processed datasets into a single dataset
combined_unemployment_data <- bind_rows(
  UNEMP_AUSTRALIA,
  UNEMP_CANADA,
  UNEMP_EURO,
  UNEMP_JAPAN,
  UNEMP_UK
)

# Rename Country_ID.x to Country_ID
combined_unemployment_data <- combined_unemployment_data %>%
  rename(Country_ID = Country_ID.x)

# Keep only the desired columns
combined_unemployment_data <- combined_unemployment_data %>%
  select(
    observation_date,
    `Unemployment Rate`,
    Country,
    Country_ID,
    US_UnemploymentRate,
    PercentageChange_Country,
    PercentageChange_US,
    RelativeDifference_Unemp
  )

# Preview the combined data
head(combined_unemployment_data)
colnames(combined_unemployment_data)
```
```{r}
#reading Gold Price 

library(readxl)
EXPORT <- read_excel("Gold Price - Export.xls", skip = 10)
IMPORT <- read_excel("Gold Price - Import.xls",skip = 10)
colnames(IMPORT)
# Rename the column IQ12260 to GoldExport
EXPORT <- EXPORT %>%
  rename(GoldExport = IQ12260)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(EXPORT)

IMPORT <- IMPORT %>%
  rename(GoldImport = IR14270)
head(IMPORT)
```
```{r}
#Calculate percentage change for Gold Export
EXPORT <- EXPORT %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Sort by date
  mutate(
    GoldExportPC = (GoldExport - lag(GoldExport)) / lag(GoldExport) 
  )

#Calculate percentage change for Gold Import
IMPORT <- IMPORT %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Sort by date
  mutate(
   GoldImportPC = (GoldImport - lag(GoldImport)) / lag(GoldImport) 
  )

#Preview the updated datasets
head(EXPORT)
head(IMPORT)
```
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
CPI_Australia <- read_excel("CPI - Australia.xlsx")
CPI_Canada <- read_excel("CPI - Canada.xls",skip=10)
CPI_Euro <- read_excel("CPI - Euro.xls",skip=10)
CPI_Japan <- read_excel("CPI - Japan.xls",skip=10)
CPI_UK <- read_excel("CPI - UK.xls",skip = 10)
CPI_US <- read_excel("CPI - US.xls",skip=10)


# Transform Dates
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  mutate(
    Date = case_when(
      grepl("Q1", Date) ~ sub("Q1", "-01-01", Date),
      grepl("Q2", Date) ~ sub("Q2", "-04-01", Date),
      grepl("Q3", Date) ~ sub("Q3", "-07-01", Date),
      grepl("Q4", Date) ~ sub("Q4", "-10-01", Date)
    ),
    Date = as.Date(Date)
  )

# Generate monthly data
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  complete(Date = seq(min(Date), max(Date), by = "month")) %>%
  mutate(`CPI rate` = approx(Date, `CPI rate`, Date, method = "linear")$y)

# Print the first few rows of the interpolated monthly data
cat("Monthly Data:\n")
print(head(CPI_Australia))

# Create a country code lookup table
country_codes <- data.frame(
  Country = c("Australia", "Canada", "Euro", "Japan", "UK", "USA"),
  Country_ID = 1:6  # Assign numbers alphabetically
)

# Add the Country and Country_ID columns to each dataset
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

CPI_Canada <- CPI_Canada %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

CPI_Euro <- CPI_Euro %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

CPI_Japan <- CPI_Japan %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

CPI_UK <- CPI_UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

CPI_US <- CPI_US %>%
  mutate(Country = "USA") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

# Check the result for one dataset (e.g., CPI_Australia)
head(CPI_Australia)

# Print the result for CPI_Canada
print(CPI_Canada)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
# Repeat for other datasets
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  rename( observation_date = Date) 

CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  rename(CPI = `CPI rate`)

# Repeat for other datasets
CPI_Canada <- CPI_Canada %>%
  rename(CPI = CPALCY01CAM661N)  # Replace LRACPTTTCAM156S with the actual column name for CPI

CPI_Euro <- CPI_Euro %>%
  rename(CPI = CP0000EZ19M086NEST)  # Replace LRACTTTTEZM156S with the actual column name for CPI

CPI_Japan <- CPI_Japan %>%
  rename(CPI = CPALTT01JPM657N)  # Replace LRACPTTTJPM156S with the actual column name for CPI

CPI_UK <- CPI_UK %>%
  rename(CPI = CPALTT01GBM657N)  # Replace LRACPTTTGBM156S with the actual column name for CPI

CPI_US <- CPI_US %>%
  rename(US_CPI = CPALTT01USM657N)  # Replace CPIAUCSL with the actual column name for US CPI

# Preview one dataset to ensure the change
head(CPI_Australia)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Calculate percentage change for Australia, Canada, and Euro
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  mutate(CPIPC = (CPI - lag(CPI)) / lag(CPI) )

CPI_Canada <- CPI_Canada %>%
  mutate(CPIPC = (CPI - lag(CPI)) / lag(CPI) )

CPI_Euro <- CPI_Euro %>%
  mutate(CPIPC = (CPI - lag(CPI)) / lag(CPI) )

# Copy the CPI column to cpipc for Japan and UK
CPI_Japan <- CPI_Japan %>%
  mutate(CPIPC = CPI)

CPI_UK <- CPI_UK %>%
  mutate(CPIPC = CPI)

# Handle US data separately, keeping only US_CPI and US_CPI_PC
CPI_US <- CPI_US %>%
  mutate(
    US_CPI_PC = US_CPI  # Copy the US_CPI column directly to create US_CPI_PC
  ) 

# Preview datasets to ensure the changes
head(CPI_Australia)
head(CPI_Canada)
head(CPI_Euro)
head(CPI_Japan)
head(CPI_UK)
head(CPI_US)


```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Add Country and Country_ID columns to each dataset
CPI_Australia <- CPI_Australia %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia", Country_ID = 1)

CPI_Canada <- CPI_Canada %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada", Country_ID = 2)

CPI_Euro <- CPI_Euro %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro", Country_ID = 3)

CPI_Japan <- CPI_Japan %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan", Country_ID = 4)

CPI_UK <- CPI_UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK", Country_ID = 5)

# Combine all datasets into one
combined_cpi <- bind_rows(
  CPI_Australia,
  CPI_Canada,
  CPI_Euro,
  CPI_Japan,
  CPI_UK
)

# Add the US CPI and US CPI percentage change to each country
CPI_US_expanded <- combined_cpi %>%
  select(observation_date) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  left_join(CPI_US, by = "observation_date")

combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  left_join(CPI_US_expanded, by = "observation_date")

# Calculate the relative difference for each country
combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  mutate(RelativeDiff_CPI = US_CPI_PC - CPIPC )

# Rearrange columns for clarity
combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  select(observation_date, Country.x, Country_ID.x, CPI, CPIPC, US_CPI, US_CPI_PC, RelativeDiff_CPI)

# Preview the combined dataset
head(combined_cpi)

```
```{r}
# Rename columns in the combined dataset
combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  rename(
    Country = Country.x,
    Country_ID = Country_ID.x
  )

# Preview the updated dataset
head(combined_cpi)

```
```{r}
#Reading Real GDP Data

library(readxl)
GDP_AUSTRALIA <- read_excel("Real GDP - Australia.xls",skip = 10)

GDP_CANADA <- read_excel("Real GDP - Canada.xls",skip = 10)

GDP_EURO<- read_excel("Real GDP - Euro.xls",skip = 10)

GDP_JAPAN <- read_excel("Real GDP - Japan.xls",skip = 10)

GDP_UK <- read_excel("Real GDP - UK.xls",skip = 10)

GDP_US <- read_excel("Real GDP - US.xls",skip = 10)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Rename the columns in each dataset
GDP_AUSTRALIA <- GDP_AUSTRALIA %>%
  rename(RealGDP = NGDPRSAXDCAUQ)

GDP_CANADA <- GDP_CANADA %>%
  rename(RealGDP = NGDPRSAXDCCAQ)

GDP_EURO <- GDP_EURO %>%
  rename(RealGDP = CLVMEURSCAB1GQEA19)

GDP_JAPAN <- GDP_JAPAN %>%
  rename(RealGDP = JPNRGDPEXP)

GDP_UK <- GDP_UK %>%
  rename(RealGDP = NGDPRSAXDCGBQ)

GDP_US <- GDP_US %>%
  rename(RealGDP_US = GDPC1)

# Preview the datasets to ensure the changes
head(GDP_AUSTRALIA)
head(GDP_CANADA)
head(GDP_EURO)
head(GDP_JAPAN)
head(GDP_UK)
head(GDP_US)
```

#converting quaterly data to montly 
```{r}
library(lubridate)
# Function to convert quarterly data to monthly data
convert_quarterly_to_monthly <- function(df, gdp_column) {
  df %>%
    # Ensure the dates are in Date format
    mutate(
      observation_date = as.Date(observation_date),
      quarter_start_date = floor_date(observation_date, "quarter")
    ) %>%
    # Create a sequence of monthly dates
    complete(observation_date = seq(min(observation_date), max(observation_date) + months(2), by = "month")) %>%
    # Interpolate the GDP values for monthly dates
    mutate(
      !!gdp_column := approx(observation_date, !!sym(gdp_column), observation_date, method = "linear")$y
    ) %>%
    select(-quarter_start_date)  # Remove the intermediate column
}

# Apply the function to each GDP dataset

GDP_AUSTRALIA <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_AUSTRALIA, "RealGDP")
GDP_CANADA <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_CANADA, "RealGDP")
GDP_EURO <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_EURO, "RealGDP")
GDP_JAPAN <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_JAPAN, "RealGDP")
GDP_UK <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_UK, "RealGDP")
GDP_US <- convert_quarterly_to_monthly(GDP_US, "RealGDP_US")


# Create a country code lookup table
country_codes <- data.frame(
  Country = c("Australia", "Canada", "Euro", "Japan", "UK", "US"),
  Country_ID = 1:6  # Assign numbers alphabetically
)

# Add the Country_ID column to each GDP dataset
GDP_AUSTRALIA <- GDP_AUSTRALIA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

GDP_CANADA <- GDP_CANADA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

GDP_EURO <- GDP_EURO %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

GDP_JAPAN <- GDP_JAPAN %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

GDP_UK <- GDP_UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

GDP_US <- GDP_US %>%
  mutate(Country = "US") %>%
  left_join(country_codes, by = "Country")

# Check the result for one dataset (e.g., GDP_AUSTRALIA)
head(GDP_AUSTRALIA)
print(GDP_CANADA)

# Preview the updated datasets
head(GDP_AUSTRALIA)
head(GDP_CANADA)
head(GDP_EURO)
head(GDP_JAPAN)
head(GDP_UK)
head(GDP_US)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Function to calculate percentage change
calculate_percentage_change <- function(df, gdp_column) {
  df %>%
    arrange(observation_date) %>%  # Ensure data is sorted by date
    mutate(
      GDP_PC = ({{ gdp_column }} - lag({{ gdp_column }})) / lag({{ gdp_column }}) 
    )
}

# Apply the function to each dataset

# Australia
GDP_AUSTRALIA <- calculate_percentage_change(GDP_AUSTRALIA, RealGDP)

# Canada
GDP_CANADA <- calculate_percentage_change(GDP_CANADA, RealGDP)

# Euro
GDP_EURO <- calculate_percentage_change(GDP_EURO, RealGDP)

# Japan
GDP_JAPAN <- calculate_percentage_change(GDP_JAPAN, RealGDP)

# UK
GDP_UK <- calculate_percentage_change(GDP_UK, RealGDP)

# US (Add specific columns for US_GDP and US_GDP_PC)
GDP_US <- GDP_US %>%
  arrange(observation_date) %>%
  mutate(
    US_GDP = RealGDP_US,  # Create US-specific GDP column
    US_GDP_PC = (US_GDP - lag(US_GDP)) / lag(US_GDP)   # Calculate percentage change
  )

# Preview the updated datasets
head(GDP_AUSTRALIA)
head(GDP_CANADA)
head(GDP_EURO)
head(GDP_JAPAN)
head(GDP_UK)
head(GDP_US)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Add Country and Country_ID columns to each dataset
GDP_AUSTRALIA <- GDP_AUSTRALIA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Australia", Country_ID = 1)

GDP_CANADA <- GDP_CANADA %>%
  mutate(Country = "Canada", Country_ID = 2)

GDP_EURO <- GDP_EURO %>%
  mutate(Country = "Euro", Country_ID = 3)

GDP_JAPAN <- GDP_JAPAN %>%
  mutate(Country = "Japan", Country_ID = 4)

GDP_UK <- GDP_UK %>%
  mutate(Country = "UK", Country_ID = 5)

# Combine all country datasets
combined_gdp <- bind_rows(
  GDP_AUSTRALIA,
  GDP_CANADA,
  GDP_EURO,
  GDP_JAPAN,
  GDP_UK
)

# Add US GDP and US GDP PC to each row without duplication
combined_gdp <- combined_gdp %>%
  left_join(
    GDP_US %>% select(observation_date, US_GDP, US_GDP_PC),
    by = "observation_date"
  )

# Calculate the relative difference
combined_gdp <- combined_gdp %>%
  mutate(RelativeDiff_GDP = US_GDP_PC - GDP_PC)

# Rearrange columns for clarity
combined_gdp <- combined_gdp %>%
  select(observation_date, Country, Country_ID, RealGDP, GDP_PC, US_GDP, US_GDP_PC, RelativeDiff_GDP)

# Remove duplicate rows if any (ensure clean data)
combined_gdp <- distinct(combined_gdp)

# Preview the combined dataset
head(combined_gdp)

```
EDA
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Create a line graph for ExchangeRate
ggplot(combined_exchange_data, aes(x = observation_date, y = ExchangeRate
, color = Country, group = Country)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Exchange Rate  Over Time by Country",
       x = "Date",
       y = "ExchangeRate
",
       color = "Country") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
ggplot(combined_exchange_data, aes(x = observation_date, y = ExchangeRate
)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Exchange Rates: USD as the Base Currency",
       x = "Date",
       y = "ExchangeRate
") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot SP500 over time
ggplot(SP500, aes(x = observation_date, y = SP500
)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "SP500 Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "SP500") +
  theme_minimal()


```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot VIX over time
ggplot(VIX, aes(x = observation_date , y = VIXCLS)) +
  geom_line(color = "purple") +
  labs(title = "VIX Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "VIX") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot Crude Oil prices over time
ggplot(crudeoil, aes(x = observation_date, y = crude_oil)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen") +
  labs(title = "Crude Oil Prices Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Crude Oil Price") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot unemployment rates with facets for each country
ggplot(combined_unemployment_data, aes(x = observation_date, y = `Unemployment Rate`)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Unemployment Rates by Country Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Unemployment Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Combine unemployment rate and US unemployment rate into one column
faceted_unemployment_data <- combined_unemployment_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`Unemployment Rate`, US_UnemploymentRate), 
               names_to = "Rate_Type", 
               values_to = "Rate") %>%
  mutate(Country = ifelse(Rate_Type == "US_UnemploymentRate", "USA", Country))

# Plot faceted graph
ggplot(faceted_unemployment_data, aes(x = observation_date, y = Rate)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = Country)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Unemployment Rates ",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Unemployment Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Faceted plot for Relative Difference in Unemployment (RelDiffUnemp)
ggplot(combined_unemployment_data , aes(x = observation_date, y = RelativeDifference_Unemp)) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Relative Difference in Unemployment by Country Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Relative Difference in Unemployment") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot GoldExport over time
ggplot(EXPORT, aes(x = observation_date, y = GoldExport)) +
  geom_line(color = "gold") +
  labs(title = "Gold Export Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Gold Export") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Plot GoldImport over time
ggplot(IMPORT, aes(x = observation_date, y = GoldImport)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgoldenrod1") +
  labs(title = "Gold Import Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Gold Import") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Faceted plot for CPI
ggplot(combined_cpi, aes(x =observation_date, y = CPIPC)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Consumer Price Index by Country Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "CPIPC (Consumer Price Index Percentage Change)") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Prepare GDP_US dataset
GDP_US <- GDP_US %>%
  rename(RealGDP = US_GDP) %>%  # Rename US_GDP to RealGDP
  mutate(Country = "USA")       # Add Country column

# Combine GDP datasets
all_gdp_data <- bind_rows(combined_gdp, GDP_US)

# Faceted plot for Real GDP
ggplot(all_gdp_data, aes(x = observation_date, y = RealGDP)) +
  geom_line(color = "purple") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Real GDP by Country Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Real GDP"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Faceted plot for RelDiffGDP
ggplot(combined_gdp, aes(x = observation_date, y =RelativeDiff_GDP)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred") +
  facet_wrap(~ Country, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Relative Difference in GDP (RelDiffGDP) by Country Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Relative Difference in GDP") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Convert the monthly data to daily frequency
combined_unemployment_data <- combined_unemployment_data %>%
  # Ensure observation_date is in Date format
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%
  # Calculate the explicit end date as the last day of December 2023 for each group
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  mutate(max_date = max(observation_date, as.Date("2023-12-31"))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Create a daily date sequence for each country, extending to 2023-12-31
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(max_date), by = "day")) %>%
  # Fill the monthly values for all days within each month
  group_by(Country, Country_ID, YearMonth = floor_date(observation_date, "month")) %>%
  fill(`Unemployment Rate`, US_UnemploymentRate, PercentageChange_Country, 
       PercentageChange_US, RelativeDifference_Unemp, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove the temporary columns
  select(-YearMonth, -max_date)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(combined_unemployment_data)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Convert the monthly data to daily frequency for the IMPORT dataset, including December 2023
IMPORT <- IMPORT %>%
  # Ensure observation_date is in Date format
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%
  # Ensure the end date is at least December 31, 2023
  mutate(max_date = max(observation_date, as.Date("2023-12-31"))) %>%
  # Create a daily date sequence
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(max_date), by = "day")) %>%
  # Fill the monthly values for all days within each month
  group_by(YearMonth = floor_date(observation_date, "month")) %>%
  fill(GoldImport, GoldImportPC, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove the temporary YearMonth and max_date columns
  select(-YearMonth, -max_date)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(IMPORT)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Convert the monthly data to daily frequency for the EXPORT dataset, including December 2023
EXPORT <- EXPORT %>%
  # Ensure observation_date is in Date format
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%
  # Ensure the end date is at least December 31, 2023
  mutate(max_date = max(observation_date, as.Date("2023-12-31"))) %>%
  # Create a daily date sequence
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(max_date), by = "day")) %>%
  # Fill the monthly values for all days within each month
  group_by(YearMonth = floor_date(observation_date, "month")) %>%
  fill(GoldExport, GoldExportPC, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove the temporary YearMonth and max_date columns
  select(-YearMonth, -max_date)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(EXPORT)

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Convert combined_cpi to daily data, ensuring December 2023 is included
combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  # Ensure observation_date is in Date format
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%
  # Calculate the explicit end date as the last day of December 2023 for each group
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  mutate(max_date = max(observation_date, as.Date("2023-12-31"))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Create a daily date sequence for each country, extending to 2023-12-31
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(max_date), by = "day")) %>%
  # Fill the monthly values for all days within each month
  group_by(Country, Country_ID, YearMonth = floor_date(observation_date, "month")) %>%
  fill(CPI, CPIPC, US_CPI, US_CPI_PC, RelativeDiff_CPI, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove the temporary columns
  select(-YearMonth, -max_date)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(combined_cpi)


```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Convert the monthly data to daily frequency for combined_gdp
combined_gdp <- combined_gdp %>%
  # Ensure observation_date is in Date format
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date)) %>%
  # Calculate the explicit end date as the last day of December 2023 for each group
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  mutate(max_date = max(observation_date, as.Date("2023-12-31"))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Create a daily date sequence for each country, extending to 2023-12-31
  group_by(Country, Country_ID) %>%
  complete(observation_date = seq.Date(min(observation_date), max(max_date), by = "day")) %>%
  # Fill the monthly values for all days within each month
  group_by(Country, Country_ID, YearMonth = floor_date(observation_date, "month")) %>%
  fill(RealGDP, GDP_PC, US_GDP, US_GDP_PC, RelativeDiff_GDP, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Remove the temporary columns
  select(-YearMonth, -max_date)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(combined_gdp)


```
```{r}
# Start with the base dataset
final_dataset <- combined_exchange_data

# Preview the structure of the base dataset
str(final_dataset)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

SP500 <- SP500 %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge SP500 data with combined_exchange_data
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(SP500, by = "observation_date")

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

VIX <- VIX %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge VIX data with the final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(VIX, by = "observation_date")

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)
```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

crudeoil <- crudeoil %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge crudeoil data with the final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(crudeoil, by = "observation_date")

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

combined_unemployment_data <- combined_unemployment_data %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge combined_unemployment_data with final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(combined_unemployment_data, by = c("observation_date", "Country", "Country_ID"))

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

IMPORT <- IMPORT %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge IMPORT data with final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(IMPORT, by = "observation_date")

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

EXPORT <- EXPORT %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge EXPORT data with final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(EXPORT, by = "observation_date")

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

combined_cpi <- combined_cpi %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge combined_cpi with final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(combined_cpi, by = c("observation_date", "Country", "Country_ID"))

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure observation_date is in Date format for both datasets
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

combined_gdp <- combined_gdp %>%
  mutate(observation_date = as.Date(observation_date))

# Merge combined_gdp with final_dataset
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  left_join(combined_gdp, by = c("observation_date", "Country", "Country_ID"))

# Preview the structure of the updated final dataset
str(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  rename(
    Date = observation_date,
    Unemp_PC = `PercentageChange_Country`,
    US_Unemp_PC = `PercentageChange_US`)
```

```{r}
colnames(final_dataset)
```
```{r}
# Reorder and rename the columns
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  select(
    Date = Date,
    Country = Country,
    Country_ID = Country_ID,
    ExchangeRate = ExchangeRate,
    SP500 = SP500,
    VIX = VIXCLS,
    CrudeOil = crude_oil,
    UnemploymentRate = `Unemployment Rate`,
    USUnemploymentRate = US_UnemploymentRate,
    GoldImport = GoldImport,
    GoldExport = GoldExport,
    CPI = CPI,
    USCPI = US_CPI,
    RealGDP = RealGDP,
    USGDP = US_GDP,
    ERPC = ERPC,
    SP500PC = SP500PC,
    VIXPC = VIXPC,
    CrudeOilPC = crudeoilPC,
    UnempPC = Unemp_PC,
    USUnempPC = US_Unemp_PC,
    RelDiffUnemp = RelativeDifference_Unemp,
    GoldImportPC = GoldImportPC,
    GoldExportPC = GoldExportPC,
    CPIPC = CPIPC,
    USCPIPC = US_CPI_PC,
    RelDiffCPI = RelativeDiff_CPI,
    GDPPC = GDP_PC,
    USGDPC = US_GDP_PC,
    RelDiffGDP = RelativeDiff_GDP
  )

# Preview the updated dataset
head(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Ensure the `date` column is in Date format
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# Filter the dataset for the date range 01/01/2013 to 12/31/2023
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  filter(Date >= as.Date("2013-01-01") & Date <= as.Date("2023-12-31"))

# Preview the filtered dataset
str(final_dataset)


```
```{r}
# Remove rows where ExchangeRate is 0
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  filter(ExchangeRate != 0)

# Preview the updated dataset
head(final_dataset)

# Check if any rows with ExchangeRate == 0 remain
sum(final_dataset$ExchangeRate == 0)

```
```{r}

library(dplyr)

# Remove rows where any column has Inf or -Inf
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  filter(!if_any(everything(), ~ is.infinite(.)))

# Preview the cleaned dataset
head(final_dataset)

# Check if any Inf values remain
sum(is.infinite(as.matrix(final_dataset)))

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Add the covid_dummy variable
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(covid_dummy = ifelse(Date >= as.Date("2019-03-13") & 
                              Date <= as.Date("2022-05-31"), 1, 0))

# Preview the updated dataset
head(final_dataset)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggcorrplot)

# Select relevant columns
columns <- c(
  "ExchangeRate", "SP500", "VIX", "CrudeOil", "UnemploymentRate", "USUnemploymentRate",
  "GoldImport", "GoldExport", "CPI", "USCPI", "RealGDP", "USGDP"
)

# Subset the data to include only the relevant columns
data_subset <- final_dataset[, columns]

# Calculate the correlation matrix
correlation_matrix <- cor(data_subset, use = "complete.obs")

# Round the correlation matrix to one decimal place
correlation_matrix_rounded <- round(correlation_matrix, 1)

# Plot the correlation heatmap with dark green and beige palette
ggcorrplot(correlation_matrix_rounded, 
           method = "square",  # Use square tiles for a clean look
           type = "lower", 
           lab = TRUE, 
           lab_size = 4,  # Increase label size for readability
           lab_col = "black",  # Set label color to black for better contrast
           colors = c("darkgreen", "beige", "darkred"),  # Custom dark green to beige gradient
           title = "Correlation Map for Selected Variables", 
           ggtheme = theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +  # Larger base size for better readability
                     theme(
                       plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),  # Centered, bold title
                       axis.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),  # Adjust axis text size and color
                       axis.title = element_blank()  # Remove axis titles
                     ))


```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(broom)  # For tidy regression summaries
library(purrr)  # For the map() function

# Perform regression for each country
results_by_country <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    regression_model = map(data, ~ lm(ExchangeRate ~ ., data = .x %>% select(-Date, -Country_ID))),
    regression_summary = map(regression_model, ~ tidy(.))
  )

# Extract and display results
results_by_country <- results_by_country %>%
  select(Country, regression_summary)

# Print regression summaries for each country
print("Regression Summaries by Country:")
results_by_country$regression_summary

```

```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ExchangeRate = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIX_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ExchangeRate"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffCPI ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ExchangeRate - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)], 
                     data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIX, ExchangeRate, CrudeOil, SP500, RelDiffCPI) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIX_lag1 = lag(VIX, 1),
      VIX_lag2 = lag(VIX, 2),
      VIX_lag3 = lag(VIX, 3),
      VIX_lag4 = lag(VIX, 4),
      VIX_lag5 = lag(VIX, 5),
      CrudeOil_lag1 = lag(CrudeOil, 1),
      CrudeOil_lag2 = lag(CrudeOil, 2),
      CrudeOil_lag3 = lag(CrudeOil, 3),
      CrudeOil_lag4 = lag(CrudeOil, 4),
      CrudeOil_lag5 = lag(CrudeOil, 5),
      SP500_lag1 = lag(SP500, 1),
      SP500_lag2 = lag(SP500, 2),
      SP500_lag3 = lag(SP500, 3),
      SP500_lag4 = lag(SP500, 4),
      SP500_lag5 = lag(SP500, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ExchangeRate) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ExchangeRate), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ExchangeRate, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ExchangeRate = !!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ExchangeRate))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ExchangeRate, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ExchangeRate, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "Exchange Rate",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ExchangeRate_", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Australia"]])

```
```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ExchangeRate = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIX_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ExchangeRate"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffUnemp ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ExchangeRate - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)], 
                     data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIX, ExchangeRate, CrudeOil, SP500, RelDiffUnemp) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIX_lag1 = lag(VIX, 1),
      VIX_lag2 = lag(VIX, 2),
      VIX_lag3 = lag(VIX, 3),
      VIX_lag4 = lag(VIX, 4),
      VIX_lag5 = lag(VIX, 5),
      CrudeOil_lag1 = lag(CrudeOil, 1),
      CrudeOil_lag2 = lag(CrudeOil, 2),
      CrudeOil_lag3 = lag(CrudeOil, 3),
      CrudeOil_lag4 = lag(CrudeOil, 4),
      CrudeOil_lag5 = lag(CrudeOil, 5),
      SP500_lag1 = lag(SP500, 1),
      SP500_lag2 = lag(SP500, 2),
      SP500_lag3 = lag(SP500, 3),
      SP500_lag4 = lag(SP500, 4),
      SP500_lag5 = lag(SP500, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ExchangeRate) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ExchangeRate), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ExchangeRate, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ExchangeRate = !!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ExchangeRate))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ExchangeRate, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ExchangeRate, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "Exchange Rate",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ExchangeRate_Lag2", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ExchangeRate_2", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Australia"]])
```
```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ExchangeRate = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIX_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ExchangeRate"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffGDP ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ExchangeRate - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)], 
                     data$Predicted_ExchangeRate[!is.na(data$Predicted_ExchangeRate)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIX, ExchangeRate, CrudeOil, SP500, RelDiffGDP) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIX_lag1 = lag(VIX, 1),
      VIX_lag2 = lag(VIX, 2),
      VIX_lag3 = lag(VIX, 3),
      VIX_lag4 = lag(VIX, 4),
      VIX_lag5 = lag(VIX, 5),
      CrudeOil_lag1 = lag(CrudeOil, 1),
      CrudeOil_lag2 = lag(CrudeOil, 2),
      CrudeOil_lag3 = lag(CrudeOil, 3),
      CrudeOil_lag4 = lag(CrudeOil, 4),
      CrudeOil_lag5 = lag(CrudeOil, 5),
      SP500_lag1 = lag(SP500, 1),
      SP500_lag2 = lag(SP500, 2),
      SP500_lag3 = lag(SP500, 3),
      SP500_lag4 = lag(SP500, 4),
      SP500_lag5 = lag(SP500, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ExchangeRate) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ExchangeRate), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ExchangeRate, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ExchangeRate = !!paste0("Predicted_ExchangeRate_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ExchangeRate))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ExchangeRate, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ExchangeRate, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "Exchange Rate",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ExchangeRate_Lag3", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ExchangeRate_3", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Australia"]])
```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIX_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 1), VIX_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 2), 
    VIX_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 3), VIX_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 4), 
    VIX_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIX, 5),
    SP500_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 1), SP500_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 2), 
    SP500_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 3), SP500_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 4), 
    SP500_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500, 5),
    CrudeOil_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 1), CrudeOil_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 2), 
    CrudeOil_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 3), CrudeOil_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 4), 
    CrudeOil_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOil, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vix_var <- paste0("VIX_lag", lag_num)
  sp500_var <- paste0("SP500_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoil_var <- paste0("CrudeOil_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ExchangeRate ~", vix_var, "+", sp500_var, "+", crudeoil_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ExchangeRate))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```


```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffCPI ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffCPI) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Japan"]])

```
```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffUnemp ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffUnemp) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag2", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_2", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Japan"]])

```
```{r}
options(warn = -1)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Metrics)  # For RMSE calculation

# Define the rolling prediction function
rolling_prediction_lag <- function(data, lag, window_size = 29) {
```
