```{r}
  data <- data %>% mutate(Predicted_ERPC = NA)  # Initialize a column for predictions
  
  # Track residuals for RMSE calculation
  residuals <- numeric()
  
  # Flag to ensure the regression summary is printed only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Loop through the dataset to predict the next day
  for (i in seq(window_size + 1, nrow(data))) {
    # Training data: Previous `window_size` days
    train_data <- data[(i - window_size):(i - 1), ]
    
    # Test data: Current day (the day we want to predict)
    test_data <- data[i, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Ensure there are no missing values in the training data
    lag_columns <- c(
      paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag),
      paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag)
    )
    if (all(!is.na(train_data[["ERPC"]])) &&
        all(!is.na(train_data[lag_columns]))) {
      
      # Train a linear regression model using the specified lag and other variables
      model <- lm(
        as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", 
                         paste(lag_columns, collapse = " + "), 
                         "+ RelDiffGDP ")), 
        data = train_data
      )
      
      # Print the summary of the regression model once
      if (!summary_printed) {
        cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag, "---\n")
        print(summary(model))
        summary_printed <- TRUE
      }
      
      # Predict ExchangeRate for the current day
      prediction <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
      
      # Add the prediction to the dataset
      data$Predicted_ERPC[i] <- prediction
      
      # Calculate residual and store it
      residuals <- c(residuals, test_data$ERPC - prediction)
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and print it
  rmse_value <- rmse(data$ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)], 
                     data$Predicted_ERPC[!is.na(data$Predicted_ERPC)])
  cat("\n--- RMSE for Lag", lag, ": ", rmse_value, "---\n")
  
  return(data)
}

# Define a function to process and generate predictions for each country
process_country_data <- function(data, country_name, window_size = 29) {
  cat("\n--- Processing Country:", country_name, "---\n")
  
  # Filter the dataset for the specific country
  country_data <- data %>%
    filter(Country == country_name) %>%
    select(Country, Country_ID, Date, VIXPC, ERPC, CrudeOilPC, SP500PC, RelDiffGDP) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
    mutate(
      VIXPC_lag1 = lag(VIXPC, 1),
      VIXPC_lag2 = lag(VIXPC, 2),
      VIXPC_lag3 = lag(VIXPC, 3),
      VIXPC_lag4 = lag(VIXPC, 4),
      VIXPC_lag5 = lag(VIXPC, 5),
      CrudeOilPC_lag1 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 1),
      CrudeOilPC_lag2 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 2),
      CrudeOilPC_lag3 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 3),
      CrudeOilPC_lag4 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 4),
      CrudeOilPC_lag5 = lag(CrudeOilPC, 5),
      SP500PC_lag1 = lag(SP500PC, 1),
      SP500PC_lag2 = lag(SP500PC, 2),
      SP500PC_lag3 = lag(SP500PC, 3),
      SP500PC_lag4 = lag(SP500PC, 4),
      SP500PC_lag5 = lag(SP500PC, 5)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Initialize the final dataset for predictions
  final_country_data <- country_data
  
  # Apply rolling prediction for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    predictions <- rolling_prediction_lag(country_data, lag, window_size)
    final_country_data <- final_country_data %>%
      left_join(predictions %>% select(Date, Predicted_ERPC) %>% 
                  rename(!!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag) := Predicted_ERPC), by = "Date")
  }
  
  # Save graphs for each lag
  for (lag in 1:5) {
    results_filtered <- final_country_data %>%
      select(Date, ERPC, !!sym(paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag))) %>%
      rename(Predicted_ERPC = !!paste0("Predicted_ERPC_Lag", lag)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Predicted_ERPC))
    
    # Plot actual vs predicted exchange rates
    plot <- ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Date)) +
      geom_line(aes(y = ERPC, color = "Actual")) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Predicted_ERPC, color = "Predicted")) +
      labs(
        title = paste("Rolling Window Exchange Rate PC Prediction for", country_name, "(Lag", lag, ")"),
        x = "Date",
        y = "ERPC",
        color = "Legend"
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
    
    # Save the plot as a PNG file
    ggsave(filename = paste0(country_name, "_ERPC_Lag3", lag, ".png"), plot = plot, width = 10, height = 6)
  }
  
  # Save the final dataset to a CSV file
  write.csv(final_country_data, paste0("Final_Predicted_ERPC_3", country_name, ".csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(final_country_data)
}

# Process data for all countries
unique_countries <- unique(final_dataset$Country)
all_countries_results <- list()

for (country in unique_countries) {
  all_countries_results[[country]] <- process_country_data(final_dataset, country)
}

# Access results for a specific country
# Example: Access results for "Australia"
print(all_countries_results[["Japan"]])
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffCPI ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffUnemp ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(plm)

# Correct lag calculation
data_with_lags <- final_dataset %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # Ensure lags are calculated within each country
  mutate(
    VIXPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 1), VIXPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 2), 
    VIXPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 3), VIXPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 4), 
    VIXPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(VIXPC, 5),
    SP500PC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 1), SP500PC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 2), 
    SP500PC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 3), SP500PC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 4), 
    SP500PC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(SP500PC, 5),
    CrudeOilPC_lag1 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 1), CrudeOilPC_lag2 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 2), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag3 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 3), CrudeOilPC_lag4 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 4), 
    CrudeOilPC_lag5 = dplyr::lag(CrudeOilPC, 5)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup after lag calculation

# Drop rows with missing values due to lags
data_with_lags <- na.omit(data_with_lags)

# Convert to pdata.frame for panel data analysis
pdata <- pdata.frame(data_with_lags, index = c("Country", "Date"))

# Function to run a model for a specific lag
run_model <- function(lag_num) {
  # Variable names for the specified lag
  vixPC_var <- paste0("VIXPC_lag", lag_num)
  sp500PC_var <- paste0("SP500PC_lag", lag_num)
  crudeoilPC_var <- paste0("CrudeOilPC_lag", lag_num)
  
  predictions <- c()  # Vector to store predictions
  true_values <- c()  # Vector to store true values
  dates <- c()        # Vector to store predicted dates
  
  # Flag to print the regression summary only once per lag
  summary_printed <- FALSE
  
  # Rolling window
  for (i in 1:(nrow(pdata) - 30)) {
    # Define training and testing windows
    train_window <- pdata[i:(i + 28), ]
    test_window <- pdata[i + 29, , drop = FALSE]  # Single-row test window
    
    # Fit the plm model
    model <- plm(
      as.formula(paste("ERPC ~", vixPC_var, "+", sp500PC_var, "+", crudeoilPC_var, "+ RelDiffGDP ")),
      data = train_window,
      model = "within"
    )
    
    # Print the regression summary once
    if (!summary_printed) {
      cat("\n--- Regression Summary for Lag", lag_num, "---\n")
      print(summary(model))
      summary_printed <- TRUE
    }
    
    # Manual prediction
    prediction <- sum(coef(model) * as.numeric(test_window[ , names(coef(model)), drop = TRUE]))
    
    # Save prediction and true value
    predictions <- c(predictions, prediction)
    true_values <- c(true_values, as.numeric(test_window$ERPC))
    dates <- c(dates, as.character(test_window$Date))
  }
  
  # Combine results into a data frame
  results <- data.frame(
    Date = as.Date(dates),
    Predicted = predictions,
    Actual = true_values
  )
  
  # Calculate RMSE
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((results$Predicted - results$Actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  return(list(results = results, rmse = rmse))
}

# Run models for each lag and store results
results_list <- list()
for (lag in 1:5) {
  model_results <- run_model(lag)
  results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]] <- model_results
}

# Step 5: Display RMSE for each model
for (lag in 1:5) {
  print(paste("RMSE for Model with Lag", lag, ":", results_list[[paste0("Model_Lag", lag)]]$rmse))
}

```
